title: Generate a Self-Signed TLS Certificate
description:
## Mint a Certificate With openssl

**Your task:** use **`openssl`** to create a self-signed X.509 certificate at **`/root/server.crt`** (with its private key at **`/root/server.key`**) whose **Common Name (CN)** is **`kubekosh.local`**.

> 💡 `openssl req -x509` can generate the key and a self-signed cert in one command. Provide the subject non-interactively with `-subj "/CN=kubekosh.local"`. Inspect it with `openssl x509 -noout -subject -in /root/server.crt`.

hints.title: One-shot self-signed cert
hints.body: req -x509 with -newkey generates a key and a self-signed certificate; -nodes leaves the key unencrypted; -subj sets the subject without prompts.

validation.description: Checks that a valid certificate exists with CN=kubekosh.local.
